what_it_does: Declares a single stack item type named 'explanation' for operator-facing explanatory content; it is read by the operator and cleared by acknowledgement, and it maps a headline field while documenting future feed and renderer surfaces.
observations: The file contains only a module docstring and one STACK_ITEM_TYPE dictionary.
It defines id, label, desc, row_fields, unsettled_state, and an open_verb fallback.
The docstring states the host should soft-degrade to the item name and fail-loud with '--unready' until a renderer exists.
It explicitly declares the type now even though feed (fork) and renderer (projection) are not yet implemented.
standouts: Declares a complete operator-cycle vocabulary entry before its supporting surfaces exist.
Documents a degradation policy: soft-degrade to name and fail-loud '--unready'.
Uses a dotted field path 'identity.headline' for the headline row field.
conventions: Docstring-first module with purpose and future-plan commentary.
Minimal data-only declaration with no executable code.
concerns: The open_verb value is a descriptive sentence rather than a concrete verb or action token.
The 'identity.headline' reference assumes an 'identity' structure not shown in this file.
The feed and renderer are referenced but not defined or imported here.
notes: A vocabulary-first declaration for an operator explanation stack item that encodes its future renderer dependency and fallback behavior.
questions: What is the exact schema of the 'identity' object and its 'headline' field?
How does the host perform the soft-degrade to 'name' and the '--unready' fail-loud behavior?
What will the feed (fork) and renderer (projection) interfaces look like?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it defines the explanation stack item type and documents future surfaces and fallback behavior.
intention_signals: It is trying to add the 'explanation' concept to a shared ledger of stack item types so hosts can recognize, minimally render, and safely degrade the item before its full renderer is built.
novelty: It encodes a not-yet-implemented renderer and feed as first-class metadata within the type declaration, making the missing surface explicit rather than implicit.
contribution: Provides a declared operator-facing explanation item type with a headline field mapping and documented fallback behavior, allowing a fused system to handle the concept even before its renderer exists.
summary_for_embedding: company/stack_item_types/explanation.py declares a stack item type 'explanation' for operator-facing explanatory content cleared by acknowledgement, mapping identity.headline to a headline row field and documenting pending feed and renderer surfaces.
intention_for_embedding: To add the explanation item to the operator-cycle vocabulary with a defined fallback and unready behavior until its dedicated renderer lands.
